Shop.title: Shop
Shop.description: Represents a business or shop using the inventory and billing system. This entity holds the core business configuration.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Shop entity.

name.description: The registered name of the shop.

currency.description: The default currency symbol used by the shop for all transactions (e.g., '₹', '$', '€').

logoUrl.description: URL to the shop's logo image, used for branding on invoices and reports.

shopOwnerId.description: Reference to the external user account that owns or manages this shop. (Relationship: User 1:1 Shop)

createdAt.description: The timestamp indicating when the shop record was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp indicating when the shop record was last updated.

Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents an individual product or service offered by the shop, including inventory details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

shopId.description: Reference to the Shop this product belongs to. (Relationship: Shop 1:N Product)

description.description: A detailed description of the product, potentially generated by an AI assistant.

price.description: The current selling price of one unit of the product.

quantity.description: The current stock quantity of the product available in the inventory. Must be a non-negative integer.

imageUrl.description: URL to the product's image for display in the inventory.

lowStockThreshold.description: The quantity level at which the product is considered low in stock, triggering alerts. Must be a non-negative integer.

createdAt.description: The timestamp indicating when the product record was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp indicating when the product record was last updated.

Customer.title: Customer
Customer.description: Represents a customer who has made or might make a purchase from the shop, storing their contact and purchasing history details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Customer entity.

shopId.description: Reference to the Shop this customer is associated with. (Relationship: Shop 1:N Customer)

name.description: The full name of the customer.

phone.description: The primary phone number of the customer.

email.description: The email address of the customer.

address.description: The physical address of the customer, used for billing or contact.

createdAt.description: The timestamp indicating when the customer record was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp indicating when the customer record was last updated.

Bill.title: Bill
Bill.description: Represents a single sales transaction, serving as an invoice record and tracking purchase history.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Bill entity (internal bill ID).

shopId.description: Reference to the Shop where this bill was created. (Relationship: Shop 1:N Bill)

customerId.description: Reference to the Customer associated with this bill. This field is optional if a bill can be created for an anonymous customer. (Relationship: Customer 1:N Bill)

invoiceNumber.description: A unique, human-readable invoice number generated for the bill, for official documentation.

billDate.description: The date and time when the bill was created or finalized.

subtotal.description: The sum of all product item totals before tax is applied.

taxAmount.description: The total tax amount applied to the bill.

totalAmount.description: The final total amount of the bill, including subtotal and tax.

status.description: The current status of the bill (e.g., 'draft', 'finalized', 'paid', 'cancelled').

createdAt.description: The timestamp indicating when the bill record was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp indicating when the bill record was last updated.

BillItem.title: BillItem
BillItem.description: Represents a single line item within a Bill, detailing a specific product sold and its state (price, name) at the time of sale for historical accuracy.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BillItem entity.

billId.description: Reference to the Bill this item belongs to. (Relationship: Bill 1:N BillItem)

productId.description: Reference to the Product entity that was sold. This links the bill item to the original product data. (Relationship: Product 1:N BillItem)

productName.description: The name of the product at the time of sale (snapshot to preserve historical data).

quantity.description: The quantity of this specific product included in the bill item. Must be a positive integer.

unitPrice.description: The unit price of the product at the time of sale (snapshot to preserve historical data).

itemTotal.description: The total amount for this bill item, calculated as quantity * unitPrice.

createdAt.description: The timestamp indicating when the bill item record was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp indicating when the bill item record was last updated.

definition.description: Represents a single shop instance. The `shopOwnerId` field in the document directly links to the Firebase Authentication UID of the owner, enabling direct owner-based authorization without `get()` calls. This is the root for all shop-specific data.

Products belonging to a specific shop. Includes denormalized `shopOwnerId` (copied from the parent Shop document) in each document for authorization independence, allowing direct `request.auth.uid` comparison for security rules.

params.name: shopId
params.description: The unique identifier of the shop.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier of the product.

definition.description: Customer records specific to a shop. Includes denormalized `shopOwnerId` for shop owner access. Also includes an optional `customerAuthUid` field (Firebase UID) if the customer has logged in, enabling customers to access their own profile and for subsequent denormalization into Bills.

params.name: shopId
params.description: The unique identifier of the shop.

params.name: customerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the customer.

definition.description: Sales transactions (invoices) for a specific shop. Includes denormalized `shopOwnerId` for shop owner access. Also includes denormalized `customerAuthUid` (copied from the associated Customer document, if available) to allow logged-in customers to view their purchase history directly without `get()` calls.

params.name: shopId
params.description: The unique identifier of the shop.

params.name: billId
params.description: The unique identifier of the bill.

definition.description: Individual line items associated with a specific bill. Includes denormalized `shopOwnerId` (copied from the parent Bill document) for shop owner access, and denormalized `customerAuthUid` (copied from the parent Bill document) for customer purchase history viewing, ensuring authorization independence.

params.name: shopId
params.description: The unique identifier of the shop.

params.name: billId
params.description: The unique identifier of the bill this item belongs to.

params.name: billItemId
params.description: The unique identifier of the bill item.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to be highly secure, scalable, and debuggable by strictly adhering to the core design principles. The central tenet is **Authorization Independence**, achieved through strategic denormalization. All data belonging to a specific shop is nested under the `/shops/{shopId}` path, with each document in subcollections carrying essential authorization context.

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
*   **Shop Owner Access:** Every document, from `/shops/{shopId}` down to `/shops/{shopId}/bills/{billId}/billItems/{billItemId}`, includes a `shopOwnerId` field. This `shopOwnerId` is a direct copy of the `request.auth.uid` of the user who owns the shop. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls in Firestore security rules to check ownership of parent documents, making rules atomic, faster, and easier to debug. For instance, a rule for `/shops/{shopId}/products/{productId}` will simply check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.shopOwnerId`.
*   **Customer Purchase History Access:** To fulfill the requirement of showing purchase history to logged-in customers, the `Customer` entity is augmented with an optional `customerAuthUid` field, which stores the Firebase `uid` of the customer if they log in. This `customerAuthUid` is then denormalized into the `Bill` and `BillItem` documents. This allows a logged-in customer to directly query their own bills and bill items (e.g., `db.collection('shops/{shopId}/bills').where('customerAuthUid', '==', request.auth.uid).get()`) without requiring expensive `get()` calls in security rules to check the parent `Customer` or `Bill` documents. This also ensures authorization rules for customer access are independent and explicit.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
This structure inherently supports Query-As-Permissions (QAPs) by ensuring that `list` operations can be securely performed without relying on security rules to filter results. 
*   **For Shop Owners:** When a shop owner queries for products or bills, their `request.auth.uid` is used in the path (`/shops/{shopId}/...`) and implicitly validated against the `shopOwnerId` field present in every document. This guarantees that owners only access data within their designated shop, allowing client-side queries like `db.collection('shops/{shopId}/products').where('shopOwnerId', '==', request.auth.uid).get()` to pass security rules.
*   **For Customers:** When a logged-in customer queries their purchase history, the denormalized `customerAuthUid` field in the `Bill` and `BillItem` documents allows them to perform queries like `db.collection('shops/{shopId}/bills').where('customerAuthUid', '==', request.auth.uid).get()`. The security rules can then explicitly allow reads if `request.auth.uid` matches the `customerAuthUid` in the document, ensuring that customers only retrieve their own bills and not those of other customers or shops.

**Structural Segregation:** Each distinct entity type (`Product`, `Customer`, `Bill`, `BillItem`) resides in its own subcollection under the `Shop` context, ensuring that all documents within a given collection share the same security posture (e.g., all documents in `/shops/{shopId}/products` are owned by the `shopOwnerId`). This simplifies security rules greatly as they apply uniformly across the collection.

**DBAC:** All authorization is based on `request.auth.uid` matching explicit `shopOwnerId` or `customerAuthUid` fields in the document data, adhering to Database-Backed Access Control without relying on custom claims.
